assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN (91% overall pass rate — eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate; evals 1, 2, 4, 5 all at 100%). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A.' The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 29-33 in 'Eval Result Detection' section: 'Eval result review correctly detected (review ID 40001) by matching all three criteria: Author: github-actions[bot] (verified), Body contains "## Eval Results" marker (verified), Body contains "sdlc-workflow/run-evals" footer (verified)'. The Test Quality row (line 13) contains extracted eval metrics: pass rates per eval, failing assertion counts. The Eval Pass Rates table (lines 38-44) shows per-eval breakdowns extracted from the eval review.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md explicitly states on line 7: 'Type: Inline review comment (NOT an eval result review)'. The classification is 'Suggestion' (line 13) based on normal review comment analysis. Lines 19-20 confirm: 'This is NOT an eval result review. The comment comes from reviewer-b (a human user, not github-actions[bot]), is an inline review comment attached to a specific file and line, and contains substantive code review feedback rather than automated eval results.' The classification references reviewer language analysis, not eval metrics. report.md line 34 also confirms: 'Human review comment (comment ID 50001, author reviewer-b) was correctly NOT identified as an eval result review and was processed through the normal comment classification pipeline.'

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row: 'WARN | Eval Quality: WARN (91% overall pass rate — eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate; evals 1, 2, 4, 5 all at 100%)'. Lines 48-56 detail the two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...'. Both assertions include evidence text explaining what was missing.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists and its line 7 states: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002. Two assertions fail because the verify-pr skill does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility for suggestion-classified review comments...' Lines 15-23 include both failing assertion texts and their evidence, matching eval-3's two failures.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 13-23 contain a '## Review Context' section with 'From the eval-3 CI review (posted by github-actions[bot]):'. Failing Assertion 1 quotes: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' with evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility...'. Failing Assertion 2 quotes: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 — it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted...'

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 10: '## Target PR' followed by line 11: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. The exact URL matches the assertion requirement.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval-3 failures traced to implement-task skill gap — convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for suggestion-classified comments'. The verdict is 'DONE' (not N/A). Lines 59-71 contain a detailed Root-Cause Investigation section with defect description, universality test, method-vs-fact test, classification as 'Skill gap — implement-task phase', and root-cause task creation details.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md, and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. All are verification/analysis outputs (report, review classification, sub-task description, acceptance criteria evaluations). No code files (.ts, .js, .py, .md skill files, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report shows the PR modifies 'style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' but these are the files being verified, not modified by the skill. The output files are all analysis/report artifacts consistent with a read-only verification skill.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows independent non-eval checks: Scope Containment (line 7): 'PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' — compares diff files against task spec. Sensitive Patterns (line 10): 'PASS | No sensitive patterns detected in added lines; PR modifies only Markdown documentation files' — scans diff for secrets independently. Acceptance Criteria (line 12): 'PASS | 7 of 7 criteria met — Check 6 scans for new symbols, verifies doc comments per language convention, produces correct PASS/WARN/N/A verdicts, Output Format includes sixth row, Step 6a mapping includes Documentation Coverage' — evaluates each criterion against diff content. The 7 criterion-*.md files each independently evaluate acceptance criteria against the PR diff content with no references to eval results or eval processing.
